title: Storage | Logos
description: Private, Secure, Decentralized Storage. Content-addressed file storage and retrieval with built-in privacy guarantees, built around the Logos Messaging Nim (LMN) node.
heading: Storage

sections.headline: Storage
sections.body: Logos Storage is a decentralised storage protocol that removes the dependency on centralised cloud providers. It enables applications to store and retrieve data — files, application state, archives, media — across a distributed network with cryptographic guarantees of data integrity and availability.

status.label: Current Status
status.body: Logos Storage is in active development, with the Codex node and storage contracts providing the foundation for durable, content-addressed data availability across the Logos stack.

sections.title: Overview
sections.description:
Logos Storage lets applications address data by content instead of location, so files and application state can be verified cryptographically after retrieval.

The protocol is designed for privacy-preserving, decentralised availability: data can be distributed across independent storage providers while applications retain integrity guarantees and avoid dependence on a single centralised cloud service.

cards.title: Sample App 1
cards.description: A storage-backed application that keeps user data portable, verifiable, and independent from centralised infrastructure.

cards.title: Sample App 2
cards.description: A decentralised archive workflow using content-addressed storage for durable publication and retrieval.
